questions.question: In what year was the Great Pyramid of Cheops built in Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid of Cheops was built around 2560 BC. Ave. Cr. and is the only surviving wonder of the ancient world.

questions.question: Who was the first Roman emperor?
questions.explanation: Augustus (Octavian) became the first Roman emperor in 27 AD. Ave. Cr.

questions.question: In which city was the first Olympic Games held in ancient Greece?
questions.explanation: The first Olympic Games were held in Olympia in 776. Ave. Cr.

questions.question: In what year did Christopher Columbus discover America?
questions.explanation: Christopher Columbus reached America in 1492. October 12

questions.question: Who invented the printing press in Europe?
questions.explanation: Johannes Gutenberg around 1440 invented the printing press with moving letters.

questions.question: In what year did the First World War begin?
questions.explanation: The First World War began in 1914. July 28

questions.question: Who led Nazi Germany during World War II?
questions.explanation: Adolf Hitler was the Führer of Nazi Germany from 1933 to 1945.

questions.question: In which year did World War II end in Europe?
questions.explanation: The Second World War in Europe ended in 1945. May 8, after Germany capitulated.

questions.question: Who was the first person to fly into space?
questions.explanation: Yuri Gagarin in 1961 April 12 became the first man in space.

questions.question: In which year did man first land on the moon?
questions.explanation: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin landed on the moon in 1969. July 20

questions.question: What year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell in 1989. on November 9, symbolizing the end of the Cold War.

questions.question: Who invented the first telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell 1876 patented the first practical telephone.

questions.question: Which medieval event between 1347 and 1353 carried off a third of Europe's population?
questions.explanation: Black Death (plague pandemic) 1347-1353 took away about a third of Europe's population.

questions.question: In what year did the French Revolution take place?
questions.explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on July 14.

questions.question: Who was the British Prime Minister nicknamed the "Iron Lady"?
questions.explanation: Margaret Thatcher was the British Prime Minister from 1979 to 1990. and earned the nickname "The Iron Lady" for her determination.
